Dear Robert Murray-Smith,

A ray diagram showing what is happening could help you understand what is happening.

replies.author: @jasonburguess
replies.text: @RobertMurraySmith  me too, great idea for sure,  I was just thinking about the more industrial version for larger more sustained energy production. Like say charging a large battery bank for use in an off grid application,  one would need to scale everything up, and therefore the stresses involved would necessitate stronger materials and larger apparatus.  In those situations it is always beneficial to consider using recycled materials and capitalization of the endless waste stream from our lives. Modifying existing parts can be helpful in cheaply making industrial grade products for our individualized needs. Maybe a video idea for you: how to make an off grid power system that completely negates the needs for grid power. Would need to include powering a refrigerator, cook top, water heater,  lights, and phone/laptop charging. Aprox 8kw continuously,  using only a battery bank and materials gathered from the waste stream,  ie: washing machine motors, lawnmowers, old cars, junk yard parts etc. I'd love to see your take on something like this.  Thanks for reading the comments and have an awesome day!

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: cheers mate

author: @stevehartley7504
text:
Years since I heard a Heath Robinson reference
I feel a flywheel would be a good addition 
Initial start up would be harder but continuous motion would be easier.

replies.author: @stevehartley7504
replies.text:
@ThinkingandTinkering 
Yes, I suppose it is. I would till add more weight, to see how little energy is needed to keep it going and how much you could get it to produce
Enjoy your stuff
I'm hoping to get a 3D printer to follow some of my own ideas and a couple of yours.
👍
